RT by @hey_itsmyturn: The IDF says it recently demolished five Hamas tunnels spanning some two kilometres in the northern Gaza Strip, on the Israeli side of the ceasefire line. The demolition operations were led reservists of the 14th Armored Brigade. The brigade has now been withdrawn from Gaza and it was replaced by the 179th Armored Brigade.

RT by @NotWoofers: The Kursk Force Grouping (commanded by the 8th Air Assault Corps) has just shared a video of their forces deploying several different systems, including a German-delivered HX-2 loitering munition to destroy a Russian 2S7 Pion howitzer inside Russia. The howitzer has reportedly recently intensified strikes on the city of Sumy and the surrounding region from Russian territory (~48 kilometres away). Apparently, it was so deep covered inside a Russian forest and well protected by not only natural barriers, that multiple bomber drones as well as HX-2 and Hornet loitering munitions were not enough to destroy the artillery piece, so they also had to strike the site several times with an M142 HIMARS MLRS. Regarding the HX-2, the German government has contracted Helsing to manufacture and deliver several thousand of these loitering munition to Ukraine, while unknown to many, Germany has also been invested in the delivery of M142 HIMARS MLRS and munitions for these systems. Germany has financed the delivery of three M142 HIMARS MLRS from US stocks with a double-digit million dollar sum in mid-2024, while new ammunition deliveries are regularly funded through NATO's PURL mechanism.
